steps.title: Calculate the slope from two points
steps.content:
With points $(-2, 3)$ and $(4, -5)$, substitute into the slope formula:
$$m = \frac{-5 - 3}{4 - (-2)} = \frac{-8}{6} = -\frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content:
Substitute slope $-\frac{4}{3}$ and point $(4, -5)$ into $y = mx + b$:
$$-5 = -\frac{4}{3}(4) + b \Rightarrow b = -5 + \frac{16}{3} = \frac{1}{3}$$
So $f(x) = -\frac{4}{3}x + \frac{1}{3}$.

steps.title: Check all choices against both points
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$f(x) = x + 5$': At $x=-2$: $-2+5=3$ ✓, but at $x=4$: $4+5=9 e -5$ ✗
  • Choice B — '$f(x) = rac{1}{2}x + 4$': At $x=-2$: $-1+4=3$ ✓, but at $x=4$: $2+4=6 e -5$ ✗
  • Choice C — '$f(x) = - rac{4}{3}x + rac{1}{3}$': At $x=-2$: $ rac{8}{3}+ rac{1}{3}=3$ ✓; at $x=4$: $- rac{16}{3}+ rac{1}{3}=-5$ ✓
  • Choice D — '$f(x) = - rac{3}{2}x + 1$': At $x=-2$: $3+1=4 e 3$ ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
